Alabama.keyNotes: Up to 10 semesters. Payer of last resort.
Alabama.vetEligibility: Veteran rated ≥40% service-connected (≥20% if rated before 5/23/2017 with pending claim). Must have 90+ days continuous active duty or honorable discharge for SC disability. Must have been permanent civilian resident of AL prior to entry into service OR current AL resident for 2+ years prior to application.
Alabama.depEligibility: Children, stepchildren, spouses, and unremarried surviving spouses. Children/stepchildren must initiate before age 26 (age 30 in some cases). Stepchildren require marriage before child's 19th birthday. For 100% P&T: special consideration as bona fide residents (5-year residency).
Alabama.coverage: Up to 10 semesters (5 academic years) for children/stepchildren of ≥40% disabled veterans. Spouses of 40-90% disabled veterans: 6 semesters (3 academic years). Covers tuition up to $400/credit hour (in-state rate) + up to $1,000/semester for textbooks and instructional fees. Does NOT cover institutional fees, non-credit courses, remedial courses, or GED prep. PAYER OF LAST RESORT — all grants/scholarships applied first.
Alabama.stacking: Payer of last resort. All other scholarships and grants (except loans) must be applied first. Can stack with Ch 35 DEA. Post-9/11 GI Bill and other federal benefits applied first.
Alabama.application: Apply online via ADVA Scholarship Portal at va.alabama.gov. Must submit FAFSA annually. Must complete FERPA release for each institution. Must maintain SAP. 8-year window from application receipt date to use semesters.

Alaska.program: Free Tuition for Spouses/Dependents of Deceased Service Members
Alaska.agency: Alaska Department of Military and Veterans Affairs
Alaska.vetEligibility: Service member must have died in the line of duty, died as a result of injuries sustained in line of duty, or been declared POW/MIA.
Alaska.depEligibility: Spouse or dependent of qualifying deceased/POW/MIA service member. Must be in good standing at a state-supported educational institution in Alaska.
Alaska.coverage: Waiver of undergraduate tuition and fees at University of Alaska system schools physically located in Alaska. Does NOT cover books, housing, or supplies.
Alaska.stacking: Can be used in conjunction with federal VA education benefits.
Alaska.application: Contact Alaska Department of Military and Veterans Affairs. Must provide documentation of service member's status.

Arizona.vetEligibility: Tier 1: Veteran who received Purple Heart AND was AZ resident or stationed in AZ at time of injury AND has VA disability rating ≥50%. Tier 3: AZ National Guard member with Purple Heart post-9/11 or medically discharged for line-of-duty injury.
Arizona.depEligibility: Tier 2: Unremarried spouse and/or child under age 30 of AZ National Guard member or Armed Forces member who was AZ resident or stationed in AZ AND was killed in line of duty or died from line-of-duty injuries.
Arizona.coverage: Tuition waiver for credits needed for undergraduate degree at AZ public universities and community colleges. Covers on-campus and online undergraduate programs. Does NOT cover books, parking, course fees, or room/board.
Arizona.stacking: Federal education benefits CAN be used concurrently with Tuition Waiver.
Arizona.application: Complete Tuition Waiver Verification Form from ADVS website. Submit with supporting documents to benefits@azdvs.gov or by mail. Must meet admission requirements and maintain SAP.

Arkansas.vetEligibility: Veteran must be AR resident at time of entry into service or official AR residence, AND declared 100% totally and permanently disabled (service-connected), KIA, POW, MIA, or killed on ordnance delivery.
Arkansas.depEligibility: Spouse (not remarried) or dependent child of qualifying veteran. Must be current AR resident. Must be enrolled or accepted at an approved AR public or private institution.
Arkansas.coverage: Covers tuition, mandatory fees, AND on-campus room and board (one of the best state programs). Coverage = unpaid portion AFTER Ch 35 DEA benefits are deducted. Limited to 8 semesters or bachelor's degree, whichever comes first. Full-time (12+ credit hours) enrollment required.
Arkansas.stacking: MUST apply for Ch 35 DEA first — MDS covers the gap. DEA is applied first, then MDS covers remaining tuition/fees/room&board. Applicants must provide proof of DEA application by June 15.
Arkansas.application: Apply online via Arkansas Scholarship Application Management System (SAMS). Required docs: DD-214, VA rating letter (100% P&T), proof of relationship, DEA acceptance/denial letter, FAFSA. Renewable annually.

California.program: College Fee Waiver for Veteran Dependents (CVFW)
California.keyNotes: Multiple plans. Plan A vs Plan B tradeoff.
California.vetEligibility: Plan A: Veteran 100% SC disabled or died of SC causes + wartime service or campaign medal. Plan B: Veteran with ANY SC disability (0-99%) or died of SC causes. Plan C: CA National Guard. Plan D: Medal of Honor recipients.
California.depEligibility: Plan A: Unmarried child age 14-27 (30 if child is also a vet); spouse of 100% disabled wartime vet; unremarried surviving spouse. Plan B: Child of any SC-rated vet, NO age limit, but income restriction.
California.coverage: Waives mandatory systemwide tuition and fees at CA Community Colleges, CSU, and UC campuses. Does NOT cover books, parking, room/board, campus-based fees.
California.stacking: Plan A: CANNOT use concurrently with Ch 35 DEA — must choose one. Plan B: CAN use with Ch 35 DEA.
California.application: Apply through local County Veteran Service Office (CVSO). Apply after tax season (May/June) for following academic year. Reapply annually.

Colorado.vetEligibility: Currently limited to dependents of CO National Guard members who were disabled or killed on duty.
Colorado.depEligibility: Children and spouses of CO National Guard members killed or permanently disabled while performing Guard duty. Must enroll before age 24.
Colorado.coverage: Tuition + on-campus room & board at CO public institutions. Up to 8 semesters within 6 years.
Colorado.stacking: Can be used in conjunction with federal DEA benefits.
Colorado.application: Apply through CDHE. Must complete FAFSA and FERPA release annually.

Connecticut.keyNotes: Vet waiver good. Dependent very limited.
Connecticut.vetEligibility: Honorably discharged, 90+ days active duty during specified wartime periods, CT resident at time of acceptance.
Connecticut.depEligibility: Dependent child or surviving spouse of Armed Forces member KIA on or after 9/11/2001 who was CT resident. OR dependent child of MIA/POW service member.
Connecticut.coverage: Waiver of tuition at CT State Community College, CT State University System, and University of Connecticut. Does NOT cover books, fees, parking, or room/board.
Connecticut.stacking: Covers tuition amount exceeding Post-9/11 GI Bill federal benefit. Can stack with federal VA benefits.
Connecticut.application: Submit DD-214 and relationship documentation to campus Veterans Affairs office.

Delaware.program: Educational Benefits for Children of Deceased Veterans / POW/MIA
Delaware.vetEligibility: Service member must have been KIA, died from service-related issues, or declared POW/MIA while a DE resident.
Delaware.depEligibility: Children ages 16-24 of qualifying deceased/POW/MIA service members. Must be DE resident for 3+ years prior to application.
Delaware.coverage: Tuition and required fees at DE state-supported colleges. Up to 4 years.
Delaware.stacking: Can be used in conjunction with federal DEA benefits.
Delaware.application: Apply online through Delaware Student Success scholarship portal.

Florida.program: Scholarships for Children and Spouses of Deceased or Disabled Veterans (CSDDV)
Florida.agency: Florida Dept of Education, Office of Student Financial Assistance
Florida.vetEligibility: CSDDV: Veteran must be 100% P&T service-connected disabled, died of SC disability, or POW/MIA. Bill Young Waiver: Any honorably discharged veteran physically residing in FL.
Florida.depEligibility: CSDDV: Dependent children and spouses. Must not already have bachelor's degree. Must be FL resident. Must attend FL public postsecondary institution.
Florida.coverage: Scholarship for tuition and fees — amount varies by institution and available funding. Bill Young Waiver: Waives out-of-state tuition differential only.
Florida.stacking: CSDDV can be used with federal VA benefits. Multiple FL waivers available — student receives most beneficial one.

Georgia.vetEligibility: GA National Guard or US Military Reservist who served 181+ cumulative days in a combat zone after 2/1/2003.
Georgia.depEligibility: Children and spouses of qualifying Guard/Reserve combat veterans. Spouses eligible only if service member is deceased or severely injured.
Georgia.coverage: Up to $2,000/year for 4 years (max $8,000 total). Applied to tuition and fees at GA public colleges.
Georgia.stacking: Can combine with GA Service-Cancelable Loan, Federal TA, HOPE Scholarship, and GI Bill. HERO is payer of last resort.
Georgia.application: File GA HERO application within 2 years of gaining eligibility. Apply through GAFutures.org.

Hawaii.vetEligibility: No state-specific veteran dependent tuition waiver program.
Hawaii.coverage: Rely on federal benefits only. UH complies with federal in-state tuition requirements for GI Bill users.

Idaho.vetEligibility: No state-specific veteran dependent tuition waiver program.

Illinois.program: Illinois MIA/POW Scholarship + Illinois Veterans' Grant (IVG)
Illinois.agency: Illinois Department of Veterans' Affairs / ISAC
Illinois.vetEligibility: IVG: Illinois veteran with 1+ year active duty during specified periods. Covers tuition and mandatory fees at IL public institutions.
Illinois.depEligibility: MIA/POW Scholarship: Spouses, natural children, stepchildren, or legally adopted children of veterans who are MIA, POW, died on active duty, have 100% SC disability, or died from SC disability. Up to 120 credit hours.
Illinois.coverage: Full tuition and some mandatory fees at any IL state-supported college or university. Does NOT cover books, room/board, or elective fees.
Illinois.stacking: IVG can be used in conjunction with federal VA benefits. MIA/POW Scholarship stacks with VA benefits.
Illinois.application: Apply through ISAC. Required: proof of veteran's status, relationship documentation, VA rating letter.

Indiana.program: Tuition and Fee Exemption for Children of Disabled Veterans / Purple Heart Recipients
Indiana.vetEligibility: Veteran with ANY percentage of SC disability (≥0%), or died of SC disability, or Purple Heart recipient.
Indiana.depEligibility: Children of qualifying veterans. Up to 124 semester credit hours at undergraduate, graduate, or professional level at IN state-supported institutions.
Indiana.coverage: Full exemption of tuition and mandatory fees (up to 124 credit hours). Covers undergraduate through professional programs. One of the few states covering graduate/professional school.
Indiana.stacking: Can be used with federal VA benefits.
Indiana.application: Apply through IN CHE. Documentation of veteran's disability rating and relationship required.

Iowa.depEligibility: Children of service members killed in action.
Iowa.coverage: Educational assistance funding for qualifying orphans.
Iowa.stacking: Can be used with federal benefits.
Iowa.application: Contact Iowa Department of Veterans Affairs.

Kansas.program: Kansas Educational Benefits for Dependents of Deceased/Disabled Veterans
Kansas.depEligibility: Spouse or child of veteran who was KIA, POW, or died of SC disability. Must be KS resident. Up to 10 semesters of undergraduate study.
Kansas.coverage: Tuition waiver at KS state-supported institutions. Up to 10 semesters.
Kansas.stacking: Can be used with federal VA benefits.
Kansas.application: Apply through Kansas Board of Regents.

Kentucky.program: Kentucky Tuition Waiver for Dependents of Disabled/Deceased Veterans
Kentucky.vetEligibility: Veteran must have been KY resident at time of death. Qualifying: KIA, died of SC disability, 100% combined rating, or died of non-service-connected causes while having wartime service.
Kentucky.depEligibility: Spouses and dependent children of qualifying veterans.
Kentucky.coverage: Tuition waiver at KY 2-year, 4-year, or vocational schools funded by KY Department of Education.
Kentucky.stacking: Can be used with federal VA benefits.
Kentucky.application: Contact Kentucky Department of Veterans Affairs.

Louisiana.vetEligibility: Veteran rated ≥90% SC disabled, or TDIU, or KIA, or died of SC disability.
Louisiana.depEligibility: Spouse or child of qualifying veteran. Child eligible until age 25 (full-time). Must be LA resident.
Louisiana.coverage: 4 years of schooling at state-supported institutions to be completed within 5 years. No age limit for surviving spouse.
Louisiana.stacking: Can be used with federal VA benefits.
Louisiana.application: Contact local Parish Veterans Service Office.

Maine.vetEligibility: Veteran rated 100% P&T SC disabled, or KIA, POW, died of SC disability, or P&T disabled who died of non-SC causes.
Maine.depEligibility: Spouse or child of qualifying veteran.
Maine.coverage: Up to 120 credit hours for associate's, bachelor's, or master's degree at ME state-supported institutions.
Maine.stacking: Can be used with federal VA benefits.
Maine.application: Contact Maine Bureau of Veterans' Services.

Maryland.program: Edward T. and Mary A. Conroy Memorial Scholarship Program
Maryland.vetEligibility: Veteran rated ≥30% SC disabled who has exhausted or is no longer eligible for federal VA education benefits.
Maryland.depEligibility: Children of veterans who are KIA, P&T SC disabled, died of SC disability, POW, MIA, or P&T who died of non-SC causes. Must be MD resident. Must be under age 24 at time of application. Must attend MD institution.
Maryland.coverage: Tuition and mandatory fees waiver (up to 5 years at a 2-year institution or equivalent at 4-year). Amount = actual tuition/fees up to the highest in-state undergraduate rate at a public 4-year institution.
Maryland.stacking: Can stack with Ch 35 DEA. Conroy covers tuition/fees; DEA provides living stipend.
Maryland.application: Apply through MHEC by March 1 annually. Required: FAFSA, VA rating letter, relationship documentation, proof of MD residency. Must maintain 2.5 GPA. Renewable annually.

Massachusetts.vetEligibility: Qualifying veteran (honorably discharged, MA resident). Full or partial tuition waiver.
Massachusetts.depEligibility: No specific dependent tuition waiver program.
Massachusetts.coverage: Full or partial waiver of tuition at MA state colleges and universities. Amount varies by institution.
Massachusetts.stacking: Can be used with federal VA benefits.

Michigan.vetEligibility: No state-specific veteran dependent tuition waiver program.

Minnesota.vetEligibility: Veteran must have been KIA or died of SC disability.
Minnesota.depEligibility: Surviving spouse or child of qualifying veteran.
Minnesota.coverage: Tuition waiver at MN state-supported institutions up to bachelor's degree.
Minnesota.stacking: Can be used with federal VA benefits.

Mississippi.vetEligibility: No state-specific veteran dependent tuition waiver program.

Missouri.agency: Missouri Department of Higher Education and Workforce Development
Missouri.vetEligibility: Veteran rated ≥80% SC disabled (disabilities MUST stem from combat), or KIA, or died of SC disability.
Missouri.depEligibility: Spouse or child of qualifying veteran. Must be MO resident.
Missouri.coverage: Tuition waiver at MO state-supported institutions.
Missouri.stacking: Can be used with federal VA benefits.

Montana.vetEligibility: Qualifying wartime veteran (MT resident, honorable discharge). For veterans whose VA benefits are exhausted or expired.
Montana.coverage: Fee waiver for associate or initial bachelor's degree at MT state-supported institutions. Up to 12 semesters.
Montana.stacking: For veterans whose federal benefits are exhausted.

Nebraska.program: Nebraska Waiver of Tuition for Veterans' Dependents
Nebraska.vetEligibility: Veteran 100% P&T SC disabled, or TDIU P&T, or died of SC disability, or POW.
Nebraska.depEligibility: Spouse or child of qualifying veteran. Must be NE resident.
Nebraska.coverage: Undergraduate tuition waiver at NE state-supported institutions.
Nebraska.stacking: Can be used with federal VA benefits.

Nevada.depEligibility: Spouse (KIA/POW) or child (KIA/POW) of qualifying service member.
Nevada.coverage: Waiver of registration fees at NSHE institutions.
Nevada.stacking: Can be used with federal VA benefits.

New Hampshire.program: NH Tuition Waiver for Children of Certain Veterans
New Hampshire.depEligibility: Children of veterans who are KIA, 100% P&T SC disabled, or died of SC disability. NH resident.
New Hampshire.coverage: Tuition waiver at NH state-supported institutions. Also eligible for $2,500/year stipend.
New Hampshire.stacking: Can be used with federal VA benefits.

New Jersey.agency: NJ Department of Military and Veterans Affairs
New Jersey.depEligibility: Children of POW service members only. Undergraduate study.
New Jersey.coverage: Tuition waiver at NJ state-supported institutions.
New Jersey.stacking: Can be used with federal VA benefits.

New Mexico.program: Vietnam Veterans' Scholarship + Children of Deceased Military Personnel Scholarship
New Mexico.vetEligibility: Vietnam-era veterans or qualifying wartime veterans.
New Mexico.depEligibility: Children of service members who died of SC causes. Up to age 26.
New Mexico.coverage: Full tuition at NM state-funded postsecondary institutions.
New Mexico.stacking: Can be used with federal VA benefits.
New Mexico.application: Apply through NM Higher Education Department (veteran) or NM DVS (dependent).

New York.agency: NY State Higher Education Services Corporation (HESC)
New York.vetEligibility: VTA: NY resident veteran, honorably discharged, served during qualifying wartime periods. Covers undergraduate and graduate programs.
New York.depEligibility: MERIT: Dependents of active duty, reserve, and National Guard members who died or became severely/permanently disabled while performing military duties. Also: Children of 100% P&T disabled veterans.
New York.coverage: VTA: Full/partial tuition at SUNY, CUNY, or eligible degree programs. MERIT: Up to tuition amount for undergrad at NY state institutions.
New York.stacking: Can be used with federal VA benefits.

North Carolina.program: North Carolina Scholarship for Children of Wartime Veterans
North Carolina.depEligibility: Children of wartime veterans: KIA, Purple Heart, POW, non-service death, ≥20% SC disabled, VA pension (P&T). Must be NC resident. Up to 8 academic semesters.
North Carolina.coverage: Full tuition at NC state-supported institutions. 4-year scholarship (8 semesters).
North Carolina.stacking: Can be used with federal VA benefits.
North Carolina.application: Apply through NC Division of Veterans Affairs scholarship program.

North Dakota.program: ND Tuition Waiver for Dependents of Deceased/Disabled Veterans
North Dakota.depEligibility: Spouse: POW, SD, 100%, TDIU. Child: KIA, POW, SD, 100%, TDIU. Must be ND resident.
North Dakota.coverage: Tuition waiver at ND state-supported institutions up to bachelor's degree.
North Dakota.stacking: Can be used with federal VA benefits.

Ohio.depEligibility: Children of veterans ≥60% SC disabled, KIA, POW, died of SC disability, or receiving VA pension (P&T). Age 16-25. Must be OH resident.
Ohio.coverage: ~79% off tuition and general fees at OH state-supported institutions.
Ohio.stacking: Can be used with federal VA benefits.
Ohio.application: Apply through Ohio Department of Higher Education.

Oklahoma.agency: Oklahoma State Regents for Higher Education
Oklahoma.vetEligibility: POW: Any state-supported technology career center.
Oklahoma.depEligibility: Children of KIA or POW service members. Must enroll before age 21.
Oklahoma.coverage: Tuition waiver at OK State System for Higher Education institutions.
Oklahoma.stacking: Can be used with federal VA benefits.

Oregon.program: Oregon Veterans' Dependent Tuition Waiver Program
Oregon.depEligibility: Spouse or child of veteran who died of SC disability, is 100% SC disabled, received Purple Heart, or PH recipient who died of non-SC causes. Must be OR resident.
Oregon.coverage: Tuition waiver at OR state-supported institutions for bachelor's or master's degree.
Oregon.stacking: Can be used with federal VA benefits.
Oregon.application: Apply through OR institution's veteran services office.

Pennsylvania.depEligibility: Children of KIA service members only.
Pennsylvania.coverage: Tuition assistance at PA state-supported institutions.
Pennsylvania.stacking: Can be used with federal VA benefits.

Rhode Island.coverage: Tuition waiver (not fees) for qualifying veterans at RI state institutions.
Rhode Island.stacking: Can be used with federal VA benefits.

South Carolina.program: SC Free Tuition for Children of Certain Veterans
South Carolina.childrenEligible: KIA, MH, PH, POW, PT, SD
South Carolina.depEligibility: Children of veterans who are KIA, Medal of Honor, Purple Heart, POW, 100% P&T SC disabled, or died of SC disability. Veteran must have been SC resident for 1+ year. Child must be under age 26.
South Carolina.coverage: Free tuition at SC state-supported colleges, universities, and post-high school technical institutions.
South Carolina.stacking: Can be used with federal VA benefits.

South Dakota.vetEligibility: Veteran with ≥10% SC disability or qualifying wartime service. Undergraduate only.
South Dakota.coverage: Tuition waiver at SD state-supported institutions for undergraduate programs.
South Dakota.stacking: Can be used with federal VA benefits.

Tennessee.keyNotes: No state program. TN Promise for all.
Tennessee.vetEligibility: No state-specific veteran dependent tuition waiver program.
Tennessee.coverage: TN Promise provides free community college for all qualifying residents regardless of military status.

Texas.vetEligibility: TX resident at entry into service OR designated TX as home of record. 181+ days active duty. Honorable/general discharge. Up to 150 credit hours.
Texas.depEligibility: THREE categories: (1) Legacy: Veteran transfers unused hours to children under 25. 150 hours shared among ALL children. (2) 100% Disabled/Deceased: Each dependent gets own 150 hours. Spouse + children of 100% P&T, TDIU, or KIA/MIA/SD veteran.
Texas.coverage: Up to 150 semester credit hours of tuition + most mandatory fees at TX public institutions. Does NOT cover books, room/board.
Texas.stacking: Can stack with Ch 33 Post-9/11 GI Bill if less than 100%. CRITICAL: Plan A (100% disabled/deceased) CANNOT stack with Ch 35 DEA — must elect one.
Texas.application: Register at TVC Hazlewood Student Hours Online database. Submit application + DD-214, relationship proof, COE to institution. Reapply each academic year.

Utah.program: Utah Veteran Dependent Tuition Waiver + Purple Heart Tuition Waiver
Utah.agency: Utah Department of Veterans and Military Affairs
Utah.vetEligibility: Purple Heart veteran: tuition waiver up to and including master's degree.
Utah.depEligibility: Child or spouse of service member KIA. Must be UT resident.
Utah.coverage: Tuition waiver at UT state-supported institutions. PH veteran: through master's degree.
Utah.stacking: Can be used with federal VA benefits.

Vermont.depEligibility: Child or spouse of service member KIA. VT resident.
Vermont.coverage: Up to 18 credit hours/semester, maximum 130 credits at VT state-supported institutions.
Vermont.stacking: Can be used with federal VA benefits.

Virginia.program: Virginia Military Survivors and Dependents Education Program (VMSDEP)
Virginia.depEligibility: Spouse or child of veteran ≥90% SC disabled, KIA, or POW. Must be VA resident. Must be accepted at VA state-supported institution.
Virginia.coverage: Tuition and fees waiver for 8 semesters at VA public institutions.
Virginia.stacking: Can be used with federal VA benefits.
Virginia.application: Apply through VA DVS. Documentation of veteran's rating and relationship required.

Washington.program: Washington State Veterans' Dependents Tuition Waiver
Washington.vetEligibility: Qualifying veteran: separate waiver program exists.
Washington.depEligibility: Spouse or child of veteran who is 100% SC disabled, KIA, POW, or died of SC disability. WA resident.
Washington.coverage: Up to 200 quarter credits of undergraduate tuition and fees at WA state-supported institutions.
Washington.stacking: Can be used with federal VA benefits.

West Virginia.program: WV War Orphans Education Program + Purple Heart/MoH Tuition Waiver
West Virginia.vetEligibility: Purple Heart or Medal of Honor veteran.
West Virginia.depEligibility: Children or spouse of veteran who was KIA or died of SC disability. WV resident.
West Virginia.coverage: Dependents: Tuition waiver + may receive $2,000/year stipend. PH/MoH veteran: tuition waiver.
West Virginia.stacking: Can be used with federal VA benefits.

Wisconsin.depEligibility: Spouse or child of veteran ≥30% SC disabled, KIA, or died of SC disability. WI resident.
Wisconsin.coverage: Tuition remission (waiver) at University of Wisconsin system and WI Technical College System institutions.
Wisconsin.stacking: Can be used with federal VA benefits.

Wyoming.depEligibility: Spouse or child of veteran who was KIA, POW, or died of SC disability. WY resident.
Wyoming.coverage: Tuition waiver at WY state-supported institutions. 8 semesters within 8-year period.
Wyoming.stacking: Can be used with federal VA benefits.

District of Columbia.keyNotes: No program. DCTAG for all DC residents.
District of Columbia.coverage: DCTAG is available to all DC residents regardless of military status.

Puerto Rico.vetEligibility: Qualifying veteran: free tuition at PR public institutions.
Puerto Rico.depEligibility: Dependents of KIA service members: free tuition. Dependents of non-KIA veterans: 50% tuition discount.
Puerto Rico.coverage: Full tuition (KIA dependents) or 50% tuition (other veteran dependents) at PR public institutions.
Puerto Rico.stacking: Can be used with federal VA benefits.
Puerto Rico.application: Contact PR Office of Veterans Affairs.

US Virgin Islands.vetEligibility: Qualifying veteran: free tuition at University of the Virgin Islands.
US Virgin Islands.coverage: Free tuition at UVI for qualifying veterans.
US Virgin Islands.stacking: Can be used with federal VA benefits.

Guam.vetEligibility: No state-specific veteran dependent tuition waiver program.

American Samoa.vetEligibility: No state-specific veteran dependent tuition waiver program.

CNMI.vetEligibility: No state-specific veteran dependent tuition waiver program.
